summary:
Routine antibiotics do not reduce post-tonsillectomy morbidity; clinical guidelines recommend against their routine use due to insufficient evidence of benefit and risks related to antimicrobial resistance. The consensus among Level I studies shows no significant reduction in complications such as bleeding, pain, or infection with preventive antibiotic therapy. 

\boxed{B}
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Routine antibiotics do not reduce post-tonsillectomy morbidity; clinical guidelines recommend against their routine use due to insufficient evidence of benefit and risks related to antimicrobial resistance. The consensus among Level I studies shows no significant reduction in complications such as bleeding, pain, or infection with preventive antibiotic therapy. 

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
